name: field
description: Form-field wrapper composing label, control, description, and error

meta.name: Field
meta.description: Form-field wrapper composing label, control, description, and error

props.name: error
props.description: Error message shown when the value is invalid.

props.name: required
props.description: When true, marks the field as required.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disables the control and removes it from the tab order.

props.name: id
props.description: Id applied to the root element (auto-generated when omitted).

examples.title: With error
examples.description: Sets aria-invalid on the control and announces the error via role="alert".

whenToUse: Pairing a single control with its label, helper text, and validation message
Wiring aria-describedby / aria-invalid automatically instead of by hand
whenNotToUse: A standalone control that needs no label, description, or error
Grouping multiple related controls — use a <fieldset> with a <legend>

antiPatterns.why: Field clones exactly one child control to inject its id and aria wiring; multiple children break the association
Hand-wired markup easily drifts: missing ids, no role="alert", mismatched aria-describedby — Field keeps them in sync

related.name: Label
related.reason: Field renders a Label and links it to the control via htmlFor

related.name: Input
related.reason: The control passed as children receives the generated id and aria attributes

intent.a11yRationale: Generates stable ids with useId and links them through htmlFor and aria-describedby; the error carries role="alert" so it is announced on appearance, and aria-invalid marks the control invalid for assistive tech

content.tone: Label is a noun phrase; error is a short corrective sentence
content.notes: Descriptions are concise helper text; errors state what is wrong and how to fix it

flexibility.note: Spacing and color must resolve to --cascivo-* semantic tokens
Any single element accepting id/aria-* props can be the child control
